findings.title: Shell Injection Risk: exec() with Template Literals in branchManager
findings.suggested_remediation: Replace all execAsync() calls that construct commands with template literals with execFileAsync('git', [...args]) (promisified execFile). Pass each variable as a separate element of the argv array. Remove execAsync and the promisify(exec) import; use execFile exclusively in this module as patchManager already does. Also extend validateBranchName to explicitly reject double-quotes and backticks as a defense-in-depth measure.

findings.title: Shell Injection Risk in createSafeCommit: commit message injected into shell command
findings.suggested_remediation: Replace the execAsync template-literal invocation with execFileAsync('git', ['commit', '-m', commitMessage], { cwd: config.workingDir }). Remove the manual double-quote replacement—it is unnecessary and incomplete when using execFile. Add input validation for the message and taskId parameters to reject embedded newlines.

findings.title: Shell Injection via exec() in patchManager.getCurrentGitRef using shell pipeline
findings.suggested_remediation: Replace the exec() shell-pipeline call with two sequential execFileAsync('git', ...) invocations. Call execFileAsync('git', ['symbolic-ref', '-q', 'HEAD']) first; if it throws, call execFileAsync('git', ['rev-parse', 'HEAD']) as the fallback. This eliminates the shell dependency entirely and aligns with the rest of patchManager.

findings.title: Unvalidated JSON Deserialization of Queue/Manifest Files Bypasses Schema
findings.suggested_remediation: For each affected site, define a Zod schema for the expected type (or reuse an existing one if available) and replace `JSON.parse(content) as Type` with `validateOrThrow(Schema, JSON.parse(content), 'boundary-label')`. The validateOrThrow helper already exists in src/validation/helpers.ts. Prioritize files that affect financial data (costTracker), authorization decisions (approvalRegistry), and PR metadata (deployment/context.ts).

findings.title: Patch Files Written to World-Writable /tmp Without Secure Permissions
findings.suggested_remediation: Use os.mkdtemp() to create a private temporary directory (mode 0700) within the run directory or os.tmpdir(), write the patch file there, and clean up the entire directory in the finally block. Alternatively, pipe patch content directly to git apply via stdin using the --stdin flag, eliminating the temp file entirely: execFileAsync('git', ['apply', '--check'], { input: patch.content, cwd: workingDir }). Pass the stdin input via the options.input parameter supported by the promisified execFile.

findings.title: Missing Input Validation on branchName Before Shell Embedding (validateBranchName Gaps)
findings.description:
--- code begin (reference only) ---
export function validateBranchName(branchName: string): { valid: boolean; error?: string } {
  const invalidPatterns: RegExp[] = [
    /\.\./,          // No double dots
    /\/\//,          // No double slashes
    /^[.\/]/,        // Cannot start with . or /
    /[\/.]$/,        // Cannot end with / or .
    /\.lock$/,       // Cannot end with .lock
    new RegExp('[@{}\\[\\]\\\\^~:?*\\s]'), // No special characters
  ];
  ...
}
--- code end ---

The validateBranchName function does not reject double-quote characters ("), backticks (`), dollar signs ($), or parentheses. These characters are required to break out of a double-quoted shell argument (e.g. `"` terminates the argument, `$()` performs command substitution even inside double quotes, and backtick performs command substitution). When branch names flow into the exec() template-literal commands in branchManager.ts, the inadequate denylist is the last line of defense before shell execution. For example, a branchName of `feature/foo" && id #` would pass validateBranchName and then inject the id command. This finding is related to finding #1 (exec with template literals) but represents a separate debt: even if exec() is replaced, the validator should be hardened.
findings.suggested_remediation: Add patterns to invalidPatterns to reject: double-quotes (/"/), backticks (/`/), dollar signs (/\$/), and parentheses (/[()]/). Alternatively, switch the validator to an allowlist approach: only permit characters matching /^[a-zA-Z0-9._\/\-]+$/ (letters, digits, dot, underscore, slash, hyphen) which is sufficient for all valid git branch name formats.

findings.title: HTTP API Responses Parsed Without Schema Validation (Unsafe Type Casts)
findings.suggested_remediation: Accept an optional Zod schema parameter in HttpClient.get/post/patch/delete methods (or in HttpRequestOptions). When provided, validate the parsed JSON against the schema before returning. Individual adapters (GitHubAdapter, LinearAdapter) can define Zod schemas for their response types and pass them to the HTTP client. For callers that do not provide a schema, return `unknown` and require the caller to validate explicitly.

findings.title: Approval Registry Loads ApprovalsFile Without Zod Schema Validation
findings.suggested_remediation: Define a Zod schema for ApprovalsFile (and its nested ApprovalRecord array) and use validateOrThrow() after JSON.parse. The existing ApprovalRecord Zod schemas in src/core/models/ApprovalRecord.ts can be referenced. The schema_version check can remain as a pre-validation fast path, but the full structure must also be validated.

findings.title: PR Metadata Loaded Without Schema Validation in Deployment Context
findings.suggested_remediation: Add a Zod schema for PRMetadata to src/core/models/prMetadata.ts and use validateOrThrow(PRMetadataSchema, JSON.parse(prContent), 'pr metadata') at this load site. At minimum, validate that pr_number is a positive integer and branch is a non-empty string before using these values in API calls.

findings.title: CostTracker and RateLimitLedger State Files Loaded Without Runtime Validation
findings.suggested_remediation: Define Zod schemas for CostTrackerState and RateLimitLedgerData and validate on load. For financial data in costTracker, add range checks: totalCostUsd must be >= 0, budget must be a positive number, token counts must be non-negative integers. Consider adding an HMAC or SHA-256 checksum of the state written at save time and verified on load to detect external tampering.

findings.title: Queue Manifest and WriteAction Lines Deserialized Without Schema Validation
findings.suggested_remediation: Add Zod schemas for WriteAction and WriteActionQueueManifest. Use validateOrThrow for the manifest and validateOrResult (logging and skipping invalid entries) for individual action lines. This aligns with the existing behavior of queueValidation.ts which already validates queue entries using Zod schemas.

findings.title: Context Summarizer ChunkMetadata Deserialized Without Schema Validation
findings.suggested_remediation: Define or reuse a Zod schema for ChunkMetadata and apply validateOrThrow after JSON.parse. At minimum, validate that required numeric fields (token counts, indices) are non-negative integers.

findings.title: patchManager.getCurrentGitRef Uses Shell (exec) with Pipe Operator
findings.suggested_remediation: Replace with two sequential execFileAsync calls: first try git symbolic-ref -q HEAD, catch the error and fall back to git rev-parse HEAD. This eliminates shell dependency and follows the pattern used everywhere else in patchManager.

findings.title: branchProtectionReporter Loads commands.json Without Schema Validation
findings.suggested_remediation: Validate commandsData using validateOrThrow with a Zod schema that constrains cmd.type to a safe character allowlist (e.g. /^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$/) before using it in string concatenation. This prevents crafted type values from producing unexpected check context strings.

findings.title: resumeCoordinator and resumeQueueRecovery Load Snapshot Without Structural Validation
findings.suggested_remediation: Add a lightweight Zod schema for the raw snapshot format (requiring checksum as a non-empty string, timestamp as an ISO string, and tasks as a non-null object) and validate before accessing fields. The existing QueueSnapshotSchema in queueStore.ts may be reusable or adaptable for this purpose.

findings.title: contextAggregator Resolves repoRoot from a Config File Path Without Bounds Check
findings.suggested_remediation: Derive repoRoot from git rev-parse --show-toplevel (using execFileAsync) rather than from the config file location. This ensures the root is always the actual git repository boundary. After resolving repoRoot, add an assertion that all discovered file paths are contained within repoRoot using path.relative() and checking for leading '..' segments.

findings.title: discoverFiles Scans Symlinks Without Checking They Remain Within Repository Root
findings.suggested_remediation: Check entry.isSymbolicLink() before processing entries. For symlinks, resolve the real path using fs.realpath() and verify it is contained within repoRoot using the same isPathContained check used in cliExecutionEngine.ts. Skip any symlink whose resolved path falls outside the repository root.

findings.title: specComposer Loads Index and Metadata JSON Without Structural Validation
findings.suggested_remediation: Define or reuse a Zod schema for the spec index format and apply validateOrThrow consistently at line 833, matching the pattern already used at lines 755 and 877 in the same file.

findings.title: taskPlanner Loads spec.json Content Without Schema Validation
findings.suggested_remediation: Define a Zod schema for the spec.json test_plan array and validate using validateOrResult (allowing graceful fallback to empty requirements on parse failure). Ensure test_id and description are validated as non-empty strings before use.

findings.title: writeActionQueue Skips Invalid Lines Silently Instead of Logging
findings.suggested_remediation: Add a logger.warn() call in the catch block with the line number and a truncated snippet of the corrupted content. Increment a metric (e.g. queue_corrupted_lines_total) for observability. Consider also adding an integrity check at load time: compare the number of loaded actions against the total_actions field in the queue manifest and emit a warning if they differ.

findings.title: Hardcoded /tmp Path Without Platform Abstraction
findings.suggested_remediation: Replace path.join('/tmp', ...) with path.join(os.tmpdir(), ...) and add import * as os from 'node:os' at the top of patchManager.ts. This is already the pattern used in the test files (validator.test.ts, RepoConfig.test.ts).
